description: Canonical rch-routed proof-command manifest for production, feature, fuzz, broad validation, rustdoc, and formal proof lanes.

resource_envelope_policy.scope_note: Proof metadata only: these envelopes classify expected remote proof-lane resource pressure and fail-closed admission semantics. They do not implement, replace, or prove OS-level worker cgroup MemoryMax/CPU quota enforcement.

classes.description: Cargo dependency graph audits should be remote-routed but are expected to consume modest memory and wall time.
Broad cargo check/build frontiers may compile large feature graphs and need explicit remote time and memory envelopes.
Broad lib/integration test frontiers can run large deterministic suites and must never silently fall back to local execution.
Workspace clippy frontiers are expensive compile-like proof lanes with strict remote-only routing.
Formatting checks are cheap but still inherit the manifest's remote-required proof-command policy.
Rustdoc/API proof lanes are compile-adjacent and require a bounded remote envelope without being treated as broad workspace health.
Lean/formal proof builds are remote-required proof lanes with explicit finite wall-clock and memory envelopes.
Contract-only lanes run focused Rust verifier tests or artifact checks and should stay bounded without inheriting broad-suite pressure.
Shell dependency audits are lightweight remote-required proof lanes with explicit no-local-fallback semantics.

validation_frontier_v2_policy.description: Machine-readable lane semantics for interpreting proof evidence without overclaiming compile-only, broad-frontier, conformance, or stale-progress outcomes.

default_stale_progress_policy.retry_policy: retry only after a deterministic stale receipt or a real compiler/runtime diagnostic

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_proof_lane_default_tokio_tree cargo tree -e normal -p asupersync -i tokio
lanes.covers: Default production normal dependency graph has no tokio edge.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Workspace, dev-dependency, fuzz, conformance, and asupersync-tokio-compat graphs are outside this production proof.
common_unrelated_blockers: Cargo.lock or dependency graph drift from unrelated feature work
lanes.escalation_notes: Any tokio path here is a production regression; file or update a blocker bead with the full cargo-tree path.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_proof_lane_metrics_tokio_tree cargo tree -e normal -p asupersync --features metrics -i tokio
lanes.covers: Optional metrics production normal dependency graph has no tokio edge.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: The fuzz feature deliberately enables OTLP generated messages through tonic/tokio and is not covered by this lane.
lanes.escalation_notes: Treat any tokio edge as a metrics production regression and capture the feature path.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_proof_lane_fuzz_tokio_tree cargo tree -e normal -p asupersync --features fuzz -i tokio
lanes.covers: The fuzz-only OTLP helper graph is expected to expose the quarantined opentelemetry-proto to tonic/tokio path.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: This is not a production-consumer proof and must not be used to weaken default or metrics no-tokio claims.
lanes.expected_signal: dependency path containing opentelemetry-proto, tonic or tonic-prost, and tokio
lanes.escalation_notes: If the path disappears, update the quarantine contract; if it appears in default or metrics lanes, file a production blocker.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_proof_lane_workspace_tokio_tree cargo tree -e normal --workspace -i tokio
lanes.covers: Workspace normal graph audit names expected scoped tokio edges in satellite crates and test/reference surfaces.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Not a default production proof; expected tokio edges are allowed when documented as scoped carve-outs.
lanes.expected_signal: tokio paths are limited to documented workspace carve-outs
common_unrelated_blockers: new workspace member enables tokio without documentation
lanes.escalation_notes: Compare output against AGENTS documented carve-outs before filing a regression.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_proof_lane_full_tokio_tree cargo tree -e features --workspace --invert tokio
lanes.covers: Full workspace feature/dev graph audit names expected scoped tokio edges in dev/test/reference, fuzz-helper, and satellite surfaces.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Not a default or metrics production proof; expected tokio edges are allowed only when documented as scoped non-production carve-outs.
lanes.expected_signal: tokio paths are explained as scoped dev/test/fuzz/satellite audit edges
common_unrelated_blockers: new dev-dependency enables tokio without documentation
new optional feature unifies into a production graph accidentally
lanes.escalation_notes: Classify every tokio path before citing this lane; any default or metrics overlap remains a production blocker.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_native_feature_smoke CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo check -p asupersync --no-default-features --features tls,sqlite,postgres,mysql,quic,http3,compression,tracing-integration
lanes.covers: Representative native optional runtime surfaces parse and typecheck together without default test helpers.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove external service behavior, broker availability, TLS trust roots, or every feature permutation.
lanes.expected_signal: cargo check exits 0 or records the first unrelated compile frontier blocker
common_unrelated_blockers: native dependency availability on remote workers
lanes.escalation_notes: If this fails before touched files compile, capture the first file/error as a validation frontier blocker.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fuzz_manifest_smoke CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml
lanes.covers: The fuzz manifest and registered fuzz targets typecheck as a cargo-fuzz workspace.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not run coverage-guided campaigns, sanitizers, or individual crash reproductions.
lanes.expected_signal: cargo check exits 0 or records the first fuzz target compile blocker
lanes.escalation_notes: For one-target work, pair this broad lane with a narrower --bin target proof if the manifest is blocked elsewhere.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_lib_tests CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --lib --features test-internals -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: Runtime crate unit-test frontier including inline module tests and test-internals helpers.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Integration tests, conformance crate, fuzz targets, examples, and benches are outside this lane.
lanes.expected_signal: lib tests pass or the first failing compile/test frontier is recorded
lanes.escalation_notes: Do not claim all-target health from this lane; use it as scoped lib-frontier evidence.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_db_feature_tests CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --lib --features postgres,mysql,sqlite,test-internals database:: -- --test-threads=2
lanes.covers: Database-feature lib-test frontier: the database:: module tree (sqlite, postgres, mysql, pool) compiled and executed under the postgres+mysql+sqlite feature set, which the standard lib-tests lane never enables (br-asupersync-uvqpga: this surface silently accumulated 26 latent failures, including a sqlite open() path-security bypass and a postgres binary-UUID decode gap).
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Non-database modules under the DB feature set, integration tests (tests/database_e2e.rs needs live servers), conformance crate, fuzz targets, examples, and benches are outside this lane. This lane is module-filtered: it is not a full-lib frontier under DB features.
lanes.expected_signal: database:: filtered lib tests pass under postgres,mysql,sqlite,test-internals or the first failing compile/test frontier is recorded
common_unrelated_blockers: rch worker roulette cold rebuild of the DB feature graph
rusqlite bundled SQLite C compile on cold workers
lanes.escalation_notes: Do not claim full-lib or workspace health from this lane; it is scoped DB-feature module evidence. A red run here is a regression against the green baseline established by br-asupersync-uvqpga/br-asupersync-vybwh6 (663 pass / 0 fail).

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_channel_mpsc_select_public_run CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo run -p asupersync --bin channel_mpsc_select_e2e --features channel-mpsc-select-e2e
lanes.covers: A focused binary target executes the channel MPSC/select E2E scenarios through public APIs without enabling cfg(test) or compiling unrelated conformance surfaces.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove the broad lib-test frontier, conformance crate behavior, examples, benches, fuzz targets, performance, or no-tokio dependency graph claims.
lanes.expected_signal: binary exits 0 after assertion-only public API scenarios or reports the first compile/runtime diagnostic
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane only as focused channel MPSC/select execution evidence; do not cite it for broad test, conformance, all-target, or release-readiness claims.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_all_targets CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo check -p asupersync --all-targets
lanes.covers: The runtime crate's lib, bins, tests, examples, and benches compile under the selected default feature profile.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Workspace satellite crates, fuzz manifest, clippy lints, runtime behavior, and feature combinations are separate lanes.
lanes.expected_signal: cargo check exits 0 or records the first unrelated all-target blocker
lanes.escalation_notes: If blocked outside touched files, cite exact file/error and use a narrower supplemental proof for local changes.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_clippy_all_targets CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo clippy -p asupersync --all-targets -- -D warnings
lanes.covers: Clippy warning-deny frontier for the runtime crate all-target profile.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove behavior, dependency graph policy, fuzz campaigns, or formal semantics.
lanes.expected_signal: clippy exits 0 or records the first lint frontier blocker
common_unrelated_blockers: warning debt in unrelated audit modules
lanes.escalation_notes: Preserve first lint file/error in Beads or validation-frontier records; avoid widening from unrelated lint debt.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_cli_feature_clippy CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=0' cargo clippy -p asupersync --features cli -- -D warnings
lanes.covers: Clippy warning-deny frontier for the runtime crate's non-default cli feature surface, which default all-target clippy does not enable.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove behavior, every optional feature combination, workspace satellite crates, dependency graph policy, fuzz campaigns, release-readiness, or broad workspace health.
lanes.expected_signal: clippy exits 0 under --features cli or records the first cli-feature lint frontier blocker
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane only for cli-feature lint coverage. It intentionally complements, rather than replaces, clippy-all-targets and native-feature-smoke.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_proof_lane_rustfmt cargo fmt --check
lanes.covers: Workspace rustfmt formatting frontier.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not compile or run tests; standalone Rust files may still need explicit --edition checks.
lanes.expected_signal: rustfmt exits 0 or prints exact formatting diffs
lanes.escalation_notes: For narrow standalone files, include an explicit rustfmt --edition 2024 supplemental proof.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_rustdoc_api CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings' cargo doc -p asupersync --no-deps --features test-internals
lanes.covers: Runtime crate public API documentation and doctest-visible examples build without dependency docs.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not run integration tests, conformance runners, or docs for every workspace member.
lanes.expected_signal: cargo doc exits 0 or records the first rustdoc warning/error
common_unrelated_blockers: rustdoc warning in unrelated public API
lanes.escalation_notes: For method-level rustdoc work, cite the method-specific proof separately if this broad lane is blocked.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- lake --dir formal/lean build
lanes.covers: Lean checks the formal core invariant project recorded in the formal proof posture contract.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove every adapter, protocol implementation, backend, or distributed transport lane.
common_unrelated_blockers: Lean toolchain or lake cache availability on worker
lanes.escalation_notes: Keep this command direct; do not shell-wrap through bash or cd.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_runtime_pressure_control_evidence_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test runtime_pressure_control_evidence_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: Pressure-control evidence artifact, docs, source schema versions, region memory-budget pressure rows, RCH proof-lane pressure rows, no-local-RCH fallback evidence, operator diagnostics bundle, scheduler pressure flamegraph attribution, deterministic lab scenario families, and operator scope limits remain aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove real-host throughput, performance improvement, scheduler regression closure, RCH fleet availability, autonomous admission/backpressure behavior, scheduler rewrites, production-on-by-default controls, or a deadlock without trapped-cycle proof.
lanes.escalation_notes: If this fails, update the artifact, docs, manifest lane, and verifier together before citing pressure-control evidence.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fourth_wave_governor_schema_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test fourth_wave_swarm_governor_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: Fourth-wave pressure snapshot and decision receipt schema fields, deterministic receipt ordering, fail-closed malformed/stale/local-fallback/advisory cases, redaction, and no-claim boundaries remain aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove the pure runtime policy engine, opt-in SLO bridge behavior, real-host throughput, scheduler regression closure, RCH fleet health, or production-on-by-default control.
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane for schema and receipt contract changes only; pair it with the policy, corpus, bridge, and benchmark lanes before making aggregate fourth-wave claims.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fourth_wave_policy_engine CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --lib fourth_wave_governor --features test-internals -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The pure fourth-wave governor policy engine deterministically maps complete, stale, malformed, local-fallback, advisory-only, no-worker, pressure-spike, optional-brownout, and admit cases to explicit receipts.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove runtime bridge side effects, benchmark improvement, scheduler regression closure, RCH fleet availability, or production-on-by-default admission behavior.
lanes.expected_signal: filtered fourth_wave_governor lib tests pass with nonzero test evidence
common_unrelated_blockers: policy receipt enum or action rename
lanes.escalation_notes: If the filter reports zero tests, treat the lane as blocked and update the command before citing policy-engine proof.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fourth_wave_swarm_replay_corpus CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test swarm_workload_scenario_corpus_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The deterministic large-host swarm workload corpus keeps fourth-wave workload dimensions, pressure scenarios, agent run scenarios, what-if scenarios, replay fields, and stable hashes aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not run fresh real-host benchmarks, prove policy-engine behavior, prove opt-in runtime bridge behavior, or claim production scheduler improvement.
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane for deterministic corpus evidence only; benchmark claims require the benchmark contract and a fresh RCH refresh artifact.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fourth_wave_runtime_bridge CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test slo_policy_bundle_contract runtime_slo_policy_bridge_fourth_wave --features test-internals -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The explicit opt-in runtime bridge preserves the base SLO decision, cancellation precedence, optional-work brownout, no-worker defer, fail-closed receipt handling, redaction, and non-claim propagation.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not enable production-on-by-default control, prove scheduler performance improvement, prove every SLO bundle path, or authorize local Cargo fallback.
lanes.expected_signal: runtime_slo_policy_bridge_fourth_wave filtered tests pass with nonzero test evidence
lanes.escalation_notes: If bridge evidence is weak, rollback by removing opt-in bridge callers and fall back to base SLO policy; do not widen the bridge from this lane alone.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fourth_wave_benchmark_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test fourth_wave_swarm_governor_benchmark_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The large-host benchmark contract preserves scenario catalog, compare modes, metric fields, log fields, RCH-only refresh commands, flamegraph attribution targets, and no-claim report boundaries.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Contract receipts are not fresh benchmark results and do not prove improvement, no regression, scheduler performance, RCH fleet health, or production-on-by-default control.
lanes.escalation_notes: Do not cite this lane as a performance pass until a fresh RCH benchmark refresh artifact is committed and classified.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fourth_wave_governor_runbook CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test fourth_wave_swarm_governor_runbook_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The fourth-wave operator runbook, README/AGENTS markers, proof manifest rows, proof-status rows, no-local-fallback language, decision taxonomy, rollback guidance, and no-claim boundaries stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not run child proof lanes, prove fresh benchmark improvement, prove broad workspace health, enable production admission control, or prove RCH fleet availability.
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane for operator-facing fourth-wave signoff text; child lanes must still be run before any fresh proof or benchmark claim.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_fourth_wave_final_signoff CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test fourth_wave_governor_final_signoff_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The final fourth-wave signoff artifact, child bead evidence rows, freshness policy, fail-closed fixtures, deterministic operator report, proof manifest mapping, proof-status aggregate row, docs markers, and no-claim boundaries stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not run child proof lanes, prove fresh benchmark improvement, prove broad workspace health, enable production-on-by-default admission control, or prove RCH fleet availability.
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane for final fourth-wave signoff aggregation only; child proof lanes must still be rerun through RCH before closing the parent epic or making any fresh proof or performance claim.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_dirty_tree_ownership_receipt CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 cargo test -p asupersync --test dirty_tree_ownership_receipt_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: Dirty-tree ownership receipt and declared-path commit preflight protect shared-main commits from mixed ownership and shared-index races.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not execute commits in the project checkout, prove unrelated proof lanes, or override Agent Mail reservation policy.
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane for ASW-7 guard changes; if it fails, do not commit from a shared dirty checkout until the first failing scenario is understood.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_durable_rch_final_signoff CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test durable_rch_proof_final_signoff_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The durable RCH final signoff artifact, child proof evidence rows, refusal matrix, operator recipes, docs markers, manifest/status mapping, main-only closeout notes, ignored-artifact force-add note, and no-claim boundaries stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove live RCH fleet availability, release readiness, broad workspace health, correctness of unrelated proof lanes, broad clippy, full release tests, or local Cargo fallback proof.
common_unrelated_blockers: durable RCH child proof row drift
mail template or e2e refusal matrix drift
peer-owned broad clippy or full-test release blockers
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane only for durable RCH proof-submission final signoff aggregation. It cannot be cited as release readiness, broad workspace health, live RCH fleet availability, unrelated proof-lane correctness, broad clippy proof, or full-test proof.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_swarm_proof_lane_planner_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test swarm_proof_lane_planner_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: Admission-aware proof-lane planner fixtures, atlas decision receipts, deterministic JSON/Markdown report goldens, docs markers, manifest mapping, and proof-status claim rows stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove broad workspace release health, conformance behavior, RCH fleet throughput, scheduler performance, or every Cargo target.
common_unrelated_blockers: manifest or proof-status snapshot mapping drift
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane for admission-aware atlas/planner/renderer changes; do not cite it as broad release, conformance, or performance proof.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_migration_readiness_planner_signoff_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test migration_readiness_planner_contract migration_readiness_planner_signoff_artifact_is_complete -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: Migration readiness planner signoff artifact, child bead evidence, fixture scenario catalog, report fields, docs markers, proof manifest mapping, and proof-status claim row stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove broad workspace release health, live external repositories, conformance behavior, RCH fleet throughput, scheduler performance, or every Cargo target.
common_unrelated_blockers: manifest or proof-status snapshot mapping drift
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane for final migration planner signoff changes; do not cite it as broad workspace, conformance, performance, or release-health proof.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_unsafe_boundary_ledger_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test unsafe_boundary_ledger_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: Unsafe-boundary ledger schema, source locator scan, stale-row detection, unledgered-site diagnostics, docs alignment, manifest row, and proof-status row stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: This lexical contract does not prove unsafe correctness, does not execute platform FFI, does not prove every cfg-gated path on every host, and does not replace category-specific safety evidence.
common_unrelated_blockers: peer-owned dirty source adds unsafe without updating the ledger
Rust syntax pattern appears outside the lexical scanner's supported surface
manifest or proof-status snapshot mapping drift
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane only for unsafe-boundary ledger drift detection and proof metadata alignment. Passing it is not a safety proof for any specific unsafe operation.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_proof_lane_manifest CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test proof_lane_manifest_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: This manifest is machine-checked for command shape, guarantee coverage, feature freshness, path freshness, and docs pointers.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not execute every lane command; it verifies that the manifest remains internally complete and repo-fresh.
lanes.escalation_notes: This is the minimum proof for edits to the manifest itself.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_rch_topology_preflight_canary CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test validation_frontier_ledger_contract rch_topology_preflight_fixtures_capture_cargo_started_boundary -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The RCH topology-preflight receipt fixtures, Cargo-started boundary classifier, proof-status blocked-infrastructure row, manifest mapping, and no-local-fallback language stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove source correctness, does not prove release readiness, does not prove broad workspace health, and does not make direct-SSH or local Cargo fallback valid proof evidence.
lanes.expected_signal: rch_topology_preflight_fixtures_capture_cargo_started_boundary test passes or emits an exact topology-preflight blocker receipt
common_unrelated_blockers: RCH generated topology-preflight shell is rejected by worker /bin/sh before Cargo starts
remote-required RCH refuses local fallback after a preflight failure
validation-frontier fixture or proof-status blocker row drift
lanes.escalation_notes: Use this lane before expensive remote-required Cargo proof lanes when RCH may be failing before Cargo starts. A topology-preflight failure is infrastructure-blocked proof evidence, not a source compile or test failure.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_rch_stale_progress_receipt CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test rch_stale_progress_receipt_contract -- --nocapture
lanes.covers: The deterministic RCH stale-progress receipt schema, fixture classifier, Markdown report, operator policy, manifest/status mapping, and fail-closed no-claim boundaries stay aligned.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Does not run live RCH, does not prove source correctness, does not prove release readiness, does not prove workspace health, and does not authorize canceling peer-owned builds.

lanes.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- scripts/detect_forbidden_quic_deps.sh
lanes.covers: ATP native core remains self-contained with no external QUIC stacks or production Tokio dependencies.
lanes.explicit_not_covered: Documented satellite crates (asupersync-tokio-compat), test features (fuzz), and conformance dependencies are outside this audit scope.

new feature accidentally pulls external QUIC stack
Tokio dependency creep in production profiles
lanes.escalation_notes: Any forbidden QUIC stack (quinn, s2n-quic, quiche) or Tokio dependency in ATP production profiles is a blocker. File immediately with dependency path.

guarantees.description: Default asupersync production normal dependency graph has no tokio edge.
Metrics production normal dependency graph has no tokio edge.
Fuzz-only OTLP generated-message tokio edge is quarantined and documented.
Workspace normal graph tokio edges are audited as scoped carve-outs rather than production regressions.
Full workspace feature/dev graph tokio edges are audited as scoped non-production carve-outs rather than production regressions.
ATP native core dependency tree contains no external QUIC stacks or Tokio runtime paths in production profiles.
ARQ/QUIC loopback E2E script has a canonical remote-required retained-artifact proof command.
ATP-over-QUIC LabRuntime deterministic contract has a canonical remote-required proof command.
RaptorQ-over-QUIC symbol-envelope property fuzz smoke has a canonical remote-required proof command.
ARQ/QUIC loopback RSS artifact validation has a canonical remote-required proof command.
RaptorQ-over-QUIC symbol-envelope wire conformance has a canonical remote-required proof command.
ARQ/QUIC fleet benchmark lane is registered with an explicit no-fresh-result boundary until G2 runs.
Representative native optional surfaces have a canonical compile-frontier command.
Fuzz manifest and registered targets have a canonical smoke command.
